item.title: The government’s hidden fees on your favorite brew
item.description: Check out our interactive to see how much you're paying.

item.title: Trump’s stock endorsements are legal, but that doesn’t make them good advice
item.description: The president is doing what anyone who wants to trade on insider information should do.

item.title: To save NATO, smile and thank Trump
item.description: The leaders’ collective top priority for the Ankara summit: Do no harm.

item.title: The U.S. needs military software. Democrats should respect the providers.
item.description: Concerns about how data is used are justified, but they should not be misplaced.

item.title: I was lucky. Other Afghan girls are still waiting for their chance.
item.description: My brothers started school in March. My sisters had to stay home.

item.title: Rhode Island decides to micromanage its grocery stores
item.description: The state's new restrictions on self-checkout will raise prices for shoppers.

item.title: What the Supreme Court got wrong about asylum law
item.description: Readers on Supreme Court decisions, foie gras and animal labor.

item.title: This little blue dot on your phone is a revolutionary invention
item.description: U.S. policy used to jam up GPS. Now, those signals beam into your pocket.

item.title: Why the transgender sports campaign was doomed
item.description: Advocates missed the critical step of building a true public consensus.

item.title: Slowly but surely, resistance to nuclear energy is melting away
item.description: Minnesota takes a step toward breaking its nuclear ban.

item.title: The evolution of cookbooks
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on how cooking has changed.
